name: Anchoring
summary: A persuasion and negotiation technique that sets the reference point for how the buyer interprets price, value, or decision stakes.
whenToUseIt: Use anchoring before serious price discussion so the buyer compares the investment against the cost of the problem, the value of the outcome, or a larger commercial context rather than a raw number alone.
steps: Establish the size of the problem, the opportunity, or the cost of delay first.
Introduce the investment only after the buyer has a stronger reference point.
Re-anchor again if the conversation drifts back to price without value.
example: Before discussing a $60K annual investment, the rep establishes that the buyer is losing more than $200K a year in wasted management time and slipped deals from inconsistent forecast control.
